failures.left_text:
Tom sails from 1 PM to 4 PM, which is a total of 3 hours. During this time, his ship travels at a speed of 10 miles per hour. The distance traveled is calculated as:

$$
\text{Distance} = \text{Speed} \times \text{Time} = 10 \, \text{mph} \times 3 \, \text{hours} = 30 \, \text{miles}
$$

To return, Tom travels back at a speed of 6 mph. The time taken to return is:

$$
\text{Time} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Speed}} = \frac{30 \, \text{miles}}{6 \, \text{mph}} = 5 \, \text{hours}
$$

Final answer: 5
failures.right_text:
Tom sails from 1 PM to 4 PM, which is a total of 3 hours. During this time, his ship travels at a speed of 10 miles per hour. Therefore, the distance traveled is:

$$
\text{Distance} = \text{Speed} \times \text{Time} = 10 \, \text{mph} \times 3 \, \text{hours} = 30 \, \text{miles}
$$

To return, Tom travels back at a speed of 6 mph. The time taken to return is:

$$
\text{Time} = \frac{\text{Distance}}{\text{Speed}} = \frac{30 \, \text{miles}}{6 \, \text{mph}} = 5 \, \text{hours}
$$

Final answer: 5
